text: Git branching is a key part of version control that helps teams work on code without interfering with each other. When you create a feature branch, you make a separate copy of the main codebase to develop a new feature or fix a bug. Once the work is finished, you can merge this branch back into the main branch. In many workflows, developers use pull requests to propose these changes, allowing team members to review the code before it is merged. This process creates a clear and organized workflow, making collaboration smoother and reducing conflicts in the code.
